title: Random forests and hyperbolic symmetry
speaker: Roland Bauerschmidt
abstract: Given a finite graph, the arboreal gas is the measure on forests (subgraphs without cycles) in which each edge is weighted by a parameter β>0. Equivalently this model is bond percolation conditioned to be a forest, the independent sets of the graphic matroid, or the q→0 limit of the random cluster representation of the q-state Potts model. Our results rely on the fact that this model is also the graphical representation of the non-linear sigma model with target space the fermionic hyperbolic plane H0|2.

proof_stages.label: Arboreal gas and external field
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines forests on a finite graph, weights them by beta to the number of occupied edges, introduces the ghost-vertex external field, and identifies the model as bond percolation conditioned to be acyclic and as the q-to-zero random-cluster limit.

proof_stages.label: Percolation problem and main phase statements
proof_stages.summary: The central question is whether a macroscopic tree exists, with the announced results being absence of macroscopic clusters in two dimensions for all beta and low-temperature macroscopic density with free-field corrections in dimensions at least three.

proof_stages.label: q=0 Potts model as a fermionic hyperbolic sigma model
proof_stages.summary: Using the Potts/random-cluster correspondence and its tetrahedral spin representation, the q=0 limit is reformulated as an H^{0|2} fermionic hyperbolic sigma model whose spin correlation equals the arboreal-gas connection probability.

proof_stages.label: Continuous supersymmetry and Ward identity
proof_stages.summary: The H^{0|2} model is shown to carry a continuous OSP-type symmetry, yielding Ward identities that play the role of the usual continuous-symmetry identities in nonlinear sigma models.

proof_stages.label: Two-dimensional reduction to a real gradient integral
proof_stages.summary: For the two-dimensional theorem, supersymmetric localization replaces H^{0|2} by H^{2|4}, horospherical coordinates and fermion integration give an exact real gradient-model integral, and Mermin-Wagner/McBryan-Spencer arguments rule out macroscopic clusters with polynomial decay.

proof_stages.label: Fermionic renormalization group in high dimension
proof_stages.summary: For dimensions three and higher, the argument stays in the H^{0|2} variables, rescales the Grassmann fields to expose a fermionic free-field term plus quartic gradient interaction, and uses fermionic renormalization group together with Ward identities to identify h=0 as the critical tuning.

proof_stages.label: Low-temperature symmetry-breaking asymptotics
proof_stages.summary: The renormalization-group analysis gives a detailed symmetry-breaking picture: spontaneous magnetization theta(beta), transverse |x|^{2-d} decay, radial |x|^{2-2d} decay, and a ghost-connection interpretation of the corresponding arboreal probabilities.

proof_stages.label: Open problems and expected extensions
proof_stages.summary: The talk concludes with open directions, including infinite-volume connectivity, upper critical dimension six, tree and finite-dimensional analogies, negative-correlation conjectures, and the unresolved search for an exact q=1 percolation field theory.
